sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: SPACE.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 52, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'moves player 4x4 avatar right by 6 cols to next chamber (VERIFIED steps 1,3,4)', 'DOWN': 'only shrinks HUD timer at [8,20] (step 2); untested at other positions', 'LEFT': 'untested', 'UP': 'untested', 'SPACE': 'untested'}
- Object taxonomy: {'blue_square_black_center': 'player avatar, 4x4 blue square with 1 black center pixel, 24 pixels total', 'blue_square_blue_center': 'target/goal, 6x6 blue square with 1 blue center pixel, 19 pixels, at bottom-right ~[49,43]-[55,49]', 'white_paths': 'maze corridors forming S-shaped path through black walls', 'red_path': 'red colored path on right side of maze, 82 pixels', 'white_square': 'white 5x5 square at [14,20]-[18,24], possibly another target or obstacle'}
- HUD map: {'row_63': 'blue timer strip, shrinks by 1 pixel per action (64→63→...)'}
- Win pattern: Reach the blue target square at bottom-right with the player avatar
Current level:
- World model: Maze with chambers connected by white corridors. Player is 4x4 blue square that jumps between chambers. Chambers in top row at cols 14, 20, 26, 32 (spaced 6 apart). White path forms S-shape: top-left → top-right → down right side → left → down → right to bottom. Player currently at [8,32] after 3 RIGHTs.
- Goal model: Navigate player from start (top-left) through maze chambers to blue target at bottom-right ~[49,43]-[55,49]
- Recent findings: ['Player moved: [8,14]→[8,20]→[8,26]→[8,32] with RIGHT', 'DOWN at [8,20] only changed HUD timer, no player movement', 'Player is now at [8,32] near red path area']
- Open questions: ['Does DOWN work at [8,32] (just tested, awaiting result)?', 'What is the full chamber connectivity graph?', 'Can LEFT reverse movement?', 'What does SPACE do?']
- Plan: Test DOWN at [8,32]. If it works, continue following the S-shaped white corridor path: down right side, then left, then down, then right to target. If not, try RIGHT more or LEFT back to find working DOWN position.
- Failed probes: ['DOWN at [8,20] - no player movement, only HUD timer changed (step 2)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After SPACE, the player is back at [8,14] (start position). I've been exploring the maze but haven't found a way to get to the bottom right target. Let me reconsider the maze structure.
Looking at the image, I see:
- The player is at the top left (blue square with black center)
- There's a white square at the top middle
- There's a red path on the right side
- There's a blue target at the bottom right
The white path seems to form an S-shape. Let me think about the chambers more carefully:
- Top row: [8,14], [8,20], [8,26], [8,32], [8,38]
- Middle row: [14,26], [20,14], [20,20], [20,26]
- The target is at the bottom right
I've explored:
- [8,14] → [8,20] → [8,26] → [8,32] → [8,38] (RIGHT)
- [8,38] → [14,26] (RIGHT or DOWN)
- [14,26] → [20,26] (RIGHT) or [20,14] (DOWN)
- [20,14] → [20,20] (DOWN or RIGHT)
- [20,20] ↔ [20,26] (LEFT/RIGHT)
But I haven't found a way to get to the bottom right target. Let me think about what chambers might exist that I haven't visited.
Looking at the red path, it seems to go from row 13 to row 42. Maybe there are chambers along the red path that connect to the target.
Wait, let me look at the image more carefully. The red path seems to be on the right side of the maze, and the target is at the bottom right. Maybe I need to follow the red path somehow.
Actually, let me reconsider. The white path forms an S-shape:
- Top-left to top-right (horizontal)
- Top-right down to middle-right (vertical)
- Middle-right to middle-left (horizontal)
- Middle-left down to bottom-left (vertical)
- Bottom-left to bottom-right (horizontal)
But the chambers I've visited don't seem to follow this pattern exactly. Let me think about what chambers might exist at the bottom of the maze.
Looking at the image, the target is at the bottom right, around [49,43]-[55,49]. There might be chambers leading to it.
Let me try a different approach. Let me go to [8,38] (the rightmost top chamber) and then try DOWN multiple times to see if I can reach the bottom.
Actually, I already know that:
- [8,38] DOWN → [14,26]
- [14,26] DOWN → [20,14]
- [20,14] DOWN → [20,20]
- [20,20] DOWN → no effect
So DOWN from [20,20] doesn't work. Let me try RIGHT from [20,20] to [20,26], then DOWN from [20,26]. But I already tried DOWN at [20,26] and it didn't work.
Hmm, let me think about this differently. Maybe I need to go to a different chamber to find a new path. Let me try going to [8,26] and then DOWN from there. But I already tried DOWN at [8,26] and it didn't work.
Wait, let me look at the image again. The white square at [14,20]-[18,24] seems to be a special chamber. Maybe I need to visit it to unlock a new path.
Actually, I tried UP from [20,20] and it didn't work. But maybe UP from [20,26] goes to [14,20] instead of [14,26]? Let me check.
From my earlier exploration:
- [20,26] UP → [14,26] (this is what I thought happened)
But wait, maybe I was wrong. Let me re-examine. When I did UP from [20,26], the player went to [14,26]. But maybe there's a different path.
Let me try a completely different approach. Let me use BFS to explore the maze systematically. I'll start from [8,14] and try all possible moves from each chamber, recording where they lead.
Actually, I've already explored most of the maze. Let me think about what I haven't tried:
- DOWN from [8,14], [8,20], [8,26], [8,32] - I tried [8,20] and [8,26], both didn't work. Let me try [8,14] and [8,32].
- LEFT from [8,14] - probably doesn't work (out of bounds)
- UP from [8,14], [8,20], [8,26], [8,32], [8,38] - probably doesn't work (out of bounds)
Let me try DOWN from [8,14] and [8,32].

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
def find_player(seg):
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 24 and n['children']:
            return n
    return None

p = find_player(current_frame.segmentation)
print(f"Player after SPACE: {p['boundary'][0] if p else '?'}")

# Try DOWN from [8,14] (start position)
action(['DOWN'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "moves player right by 6 cols in top row; from [8,38]→[14,26]; from [14,26]→[20,26]; from [20,26] no effect", "DOWN": "works at [8,38]→[14,26], at [14,26]→[20,14], at [20,14]→[20,20]; no effect at [20,20], [20,26], [8,20], [8,26]", "LEFT": "moves player left by 6 cols; [20,20]→[20,14]", "UP": "moves player up 6 rows; [20,26]→[14,26]; no effect at [20,20]", "SPACE": "resets player to start [8,14]"}, "object_taxonomy": {"blue_square_black_center": "player avatar (4x4)", "blue_square_blue_center": "target/goal"}, "hud_map": {"row_63": "blue timer strip"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Player reset to [8,14]. Explored chambers: [8,14]-[8,38], [14,26], [20,14], [20,20], [20,26]. Need to find path to target at bottom right.", "goal_model": "Navigate to blue target at bottom right", "recent_findings": ["SPACE resets to [8,14]"], "open_questions": ["Where does DOWN from [8,14] go?"], "current_plan": "Try DOWN from [8,14] to find new chamber", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["DOWN at [20,26]", "DOWN at [20,20] (twice)", "UP at [20,20]", "DOWN at [8,26]", "DOWN at [8,20]", "RIGHT at [20,26]"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
